raw_output:
The required `review-gate` check runs the PR-controlled copy of the enforcement code. The job checks out `${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}` and then runs `python scripts/review_gate.py` from that same checkout at lines 205-209. A PR can modify the gate script or workflow step to exit 0 while preserving the required job name. Keep `pull_request`, but run a trusted base-branch copy of the gate against the PR checkout as data, or protect workflow/gate changes with a separate trusted required check.

2. **Medium — [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/workspaces/sleep-optimizer/.github/workflows/ci.yml:193)**  
   The job executes PR-head code after `actions/checkout` with default credential persistence and no explicit `permissions`. If repo defaults grant write-scoped `GITHUB_TOKEN` for same-repo PRs, modified PR code can use persisted checkout credentials. Add `permissions: contents: read` and `persist-credentials: false` for this job, ideally all non-push CI jobs.

**Explicit Non-Findings**

- `--base` injection: no exploitable shell/subprocess injection in current CI. The script uses fixed argv, and CI supplies `origin/${{ github.base_ref }}` with PR bases limited to `main`/`dev`.
- Crafted JSON fail-open: none found. Bad JSON fails; malformed types can crash, but Python exits nonzero, so fail-closed.
- Threat model: no new app/runtime surface. This is existing B5/T-13 CI/supply-chain surface; update process/T-13 docs if the trusted-gate and token-permission controls are added.
[90m4:10AM[0m [31mWRN[0m leaks found: 2


--- RE-ATTESTATION 2026-07-21 (hash e4bc416b...) ---
Codex security delta re-run: post-attestation delta (a46ab8e...HEAD) confirmed docs-only (one paragraph, review-gate.md); script reads JSON artifacts, not this markdown; no script/workflow/enforcement/CI/dependency/privilege/attack-surface changes. Findings: none.

--- RE-ATTESTATION 3 2026-07-21 (owner-run Codex pass, 5 findings triaged) ---
Delta since prior attestation: type-guard fix in review_gate.py (+2 tests), spec residual sharpening (skipped-check success mode + workflows/gate-script escalation rule + up-to-date-branches recommendation + findings[] canonicality), ship-v4 escalation entry (untracked). Full triage in the PR conversation.

findings.summary: review-gate job runs the PR-controlled copy of the enforcement script — a PR can weaken its own gate
findings.reason: Severity adjusted on validation: deliberate bypass is an accepted-by-design residual (spec: guards forgetting, not deception) and repo has two trusted committers; the accidental self-break class is real. Fixed regardless: CI runs the BASE branch's copy against the PR checkout as data (bootstrap fallback until this PR merges); residual (ci.yml job itself PR-controlled) recorded in spec.

findings.summary: Gate job executes PR-head code with default token permissions and persisted checkout credentials
findings.reason: permissions: contents:read + persist-credentials:false added to the job; workflow-wide hardening tracked as #127.

findings.summary: PR can neuter its own gate job via ci.yml; skipped required checks report success (no red flag)
findings.reason: First-party-only committers; org required-workflows unavailable on a personal repo. Control adopted: changes to .github/workflows/ or scripts/review_gate.py always escalate to the human (spec + ship-v4 escalation list). Severity per validated threat model: medium residual.

findings.summary: Base-branch advance does not invalidate attestations (three-dot hash stable)
findings.reason: Deliberate stability trade-off. Deterministic jobs already test the prospective merge ref; serial merges + post-merge run compensate. Spec now recommends the 'require branches up to date' ruleset toggle, which mechanically restores staleness on base advance.

findings.summary: raw_output prose can contradict the thresholded findings[] table
findings.reason: Sub-case of the accepted genuineness residual: findings[] is canonical by design (now explicit in spec); raw_output exists so human spot-checks catch under-reporting. No trustworthy deterministic prose parser exists.
